close_reason: Fixed by using stateful mock store that tracks puts and returns them on gets
description:
Store.complete tests fail because expect_lease_get returns a pre-created lease with a different UUID than what Store.obtain_lease actually creates. When Store.complete checks stored_lease.id == lease.id, they don't match and it returns {:error, :lease_mismatch}. 

Failing tests (all same root cause):
- happy path calls Store.complete when job returns :ok
- happy path calls Store.complete when job returns {:ok, result}
- discard handling calls Store.complete when job returns {:discard, reason}
- missing handler discards job when no handler registered for topic

Fix options:
1. Use stubs for reads and expects only for writes
2. Store the lease from put expectation and return it in get expectation
3. Mock at Store level instead of Repo level
title: Fix lease ID mismatch in integration tests
